1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and management of the document.  
   Expected Content: Title, versioning, approval details, and revision history.  
   Sample Elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Governance Scope Document  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the governance scope and its significance.  
   Expected Content: Summary of the governance principles, scope, and objectives.  
   Sample Elements: Overview of foundational governance principles and alignment with strategic objectives.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the governance domain and contextualize the document within the organization.  
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample Elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Governance  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data management landscape.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clarify the specific action being addressed by the governance scope document.  
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample Elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DG-01 - Define foundational governance principles and scope  
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish foundational governance principles aligned with strategic objectives.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Define the limits of governance responsibilities and themes.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.  
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.  
   Sample Elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: Define governance principles and scope.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Identification of governance themes.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Governance scope document.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Identify Governance Themes  
   - Define Enterprise-Level Scope  
   - Define Domain-Level Scope  
   - Establish Governance Principles  
   - Confirm Stewardship Alignment  
   - Define Decision Authority and Responsibilities  
   - Review and Validate Governance Scope Document  
   - Communicate and Implement Governance Scope  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To detail the components that constitute the governance framework.  
   Expected Content: Coverage areas, themes, organizational structure, and decision authority.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All data management functions within the organization.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets relevant to governance themes.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, data privacy, compliance.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for governance decisions.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Conduct workshops with stakeholders.  
   - Document identified themes.  
   - Outline enterprise-level governance scope.  
   - Validate domain-level scopes with experts.  
   - Develop governance principles.  
   - Assign data stewards.  
   - Outline decision-making authority.  
   - Review governance scope document.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with other governance frameworks.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this governance scope.  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To establish the mechanisms for monitoring and compliance of the governance framework.  
   Expected Content: Monitoring, compliance alignment, and escalation pathways.  
   Sample Elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Conceptual monitoring of governance adherence.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Reference to internal audit function for compliance verification.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Defined pathways for escalating governance issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Not applicable (Score 0).  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the governance scope document.  
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycles, and document ownership.  
   Sample Elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Process for document updates and revisions.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Frequency of document reviews.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Assigned ownership to the Data Governance Office.  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references that support the governance scope document.  
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample Elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Governance scope document template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK2 framework references.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Data Capability Assessment Model (DCAM) documentation.

1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the foundational metadata for the governance document.  
   Expected content: Title, versioning, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Initial Data Governance Policy  
   1.2 Version and Status: v0.1 - Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, description of changes, author.

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected content: Summary of the governance principles, scope, and objectives.  
   Sample elements: This document outlines the foundational principles of Data Governance, establishing a framework for data stewardship and accountability within the organization.

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the specific domain of governance and contextualize its relevance.  
   Expected content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Governance (DG)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data landscape and governance needs.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clarify the specific action being undertaken within the governance framework.  
   Expected content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DG-01 - Define foundational governance principles and scope  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a clear framework for data governance.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Identification of data domains and stakeholders.

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To detail the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.  
   Expected content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and step-by-step execution structure.  
   Sample elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To draft an initial Data Governance policy.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires a scope defined.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Policy draft v0.1.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Define the scope of Data Governance.  
   - Draft the purpose statement of the Data Governance policy.  
   - Establish the authority section of the policy.  
   - Develop the escalation procedures.  
   - Incorporate accountability and decision transparency principles.  
   - Structure the policy using DAMA’s artefact guidance.  
   - Review and refine the policy draft.  
   - Obtain approval from governance authorities.

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To outline the structural elements that support the governance framework.  
   Expected content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All data domains within the organization.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets managed under the governance framework.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Accountability, transparency, and stewardship.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council and Data Governance Office.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles of Data Owners and Data Stewards.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Establish data ownership.  
   - Define data stewardship responsibilities.  
   - Implement data quality controls.  
   - Monitor compliance with governance policies.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with other governance frameworks as necessary.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this governance policy.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To define the mechanisms for monitoring and ensuring compliance with the governance policy.  
   Expected content: Monitoring, compliance alignment, and escalation model.  
   Sample elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Establishment of basic monitoring mechanisms.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Reference to internal audit function for compliance verification.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Definition of escalation pathways for governance issues.

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the governance document.  
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and document ownership.  
   Sample elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Procedures for document updates and revisions.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review of the governance policy.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Assigned to the Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the governance document.  
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Sample governance policy template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Related governance documents and frameworks.

1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and management of the document.  
   Expected content: Title, versioning, approval details, and revision history.  
   Sample elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Initial Governance Role List  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected content: Summary of the governance principles, roles, and the importance of establishing a governance framework.  
   Sample elements: This document outlines the foundational governance principles and the initial governance role list necessary for effective data governance within the organization.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the specific domain of governance and contextualize the document within the organization.  
   Expected content: Definitions of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.  
   Sample elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Governance (DG)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data management landscape.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken to establish governance principles.  
   Expected content: Action code, title, objectives, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DG-01 - Define foundational governance principles and scope  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a clear framework for data governance roles.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all data governance activities within the organization.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objectives.  
   Expected content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and a detailed step-by-step execution structure.  
   Sample elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To identify and classify preliminary governance roles.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires mapped stakeholders.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Initial governance role list.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Map Stakeholders  
   - Define Governance Roles  
   - Assign Responsibilities  
   - Validate Role Coverage  
   - Confirm Role Clarity  
   - Assess Behavioral Readiness  
   - Finalize Initial Governance Role List  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To detail the components that make up the governance structure.  
   Expected content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Identification of all relevant stakeholders.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: Overview of data assets governed.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Key themes guiding governance efforts.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Diagram of governance roles and relationships.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Description of decision-making authority.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Stakeholder mapping  
   - Role definition  
   - Responsibility assignment  
   - Role validation  
   - Clarity confirmation  
   - Readiness assessment  
   - Finalization of role list  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with existing governance frameworks.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this governance framework.  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To outline the mechanisms for monitoring and ensuring compliance with governance principles.  
   Expected content: Monitoring strategies, compliance alignment, and escalation pathways.  
   Sample elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Overview of monitoring strategies (conceptual only).  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Description of alignment with internal audit functions.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Outline of the formal escalation pathways for governance issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Not applicable (Score 0).  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To define the management and oversight of the document itself.  
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycles, and document ownership.  
   Sample elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Description of how the document will be maintained.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Frequency of document reviews.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Designation of the Data Governance Office as the owner.  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.  
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Governance role definition template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK reference.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Stakeholder mapping tools.

   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected Content: Summary of the governance model optimization process and its importance to the organization.  
   Sample Elements: Overview of the need for optimization, key objectives, and expected outcomes.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the scope and relevance of the governance model within the organization.  
   Expected Content: Definitions and context for the governance domain and maturity positioning.  
   Sample Elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Governance  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data management landscape.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 5 - Optimized  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being taken to optimize the governance model.  
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objectives, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample Elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DG-09, Optimize governance model and institutionalize best practices  
   4.2 Action Objective: To enhance the governance operating model through best practices.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all data governance activities across the organization.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To outline the steps necessary to execute the optimization of the governance model.  
   Expected Content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.  
   Sample Elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To continuously optimize the governance operating model.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires a model review cycle.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Governance operating model v3.0.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Initiate Model Review Cycle  
   - Assess Current Governance Model  
   - Refine Governance Structures  
   - Validate Alignment with Stewardship Framework  
   - Incorporate Metadata and Quality Expectations  
   - Align with DCAM Capability Maturity Indicators  
   - Finalize Governance Operating Model  
   - Communicate and Institutionalize Best Practices  
   - Establish Governance Checkpoints  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To define the organizational and structural elements of the governance model.  
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments involved in data management.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All critical data assets managed by the organization.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, metadata management, stewardship.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Diagram of governance roles and relationships.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Roles and responsibilities of the Data Governance Council, Data Owners, and Data Stewards.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Establish governance policies  
   - Monitor data quality  
   - Review data stewardship roles  
   - Conduct training sessions  
   - Evaluate compliance with governance standards  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with risk management and compliance frameworks.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by the governance model, such as non-data related processes.

   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Description of how the governance model aligns with internal audit functions.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Defined pathways for escalating governance issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Documentation of continuous improvement initiatives and their impact on business value (mandatory for Score 5).
